extra.reasoning: stiff_neck appears in no individual rule; its only effect is as one of the ten conjuncts of r10 (priority 100, the highest), which sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). Consider the baseline where the other nine conjuncts of r10 are all true (hemoptysis, fever, sweating, abdominal_pain, leg_swelling, chest_pain, palpitations, severe_headache, back_pain) and stiff_neck = false. Then r10's condition fails, so the highest-priority matching rule is a priority-3 emergent rule (e.g., r1 fever, r6 severe_headache, r7 abdominal_pain, r9 chest_pain) -> acuity = emergent. Now flip stiff_neck to true with everything else unchanged: all ten conjuncts of r10 are now true, r10 fires at priority 100 (beating every priority-3 rule) -> acuity = self_care. Thus setting stiff_neck true LOWERS acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness is the stiff_neck=true assignment producing self_care; its counterpart with stiff_neck=false produces emergent. (syncope is irrelevant: it only triggers r3 -> urgent, which is still dominated by the priority-3 emergent rules when false and by r10 when stiff_neck is true.)
